coredata.dc:title: Preparation and Electrochemical Performance of Spherical Mesophase Soft Carbon Materials Composite Anodes for Lithium-ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2019 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Lin, Hao
Zhao, Qinghai
Zhang, Hongxin
coredata.dc:description: It is imperative to develop lithium battery anode materials with high power performance. In this paper, by improving the preparation process, a novel micro-nano composite structure spherical mesophase soft carbon material was developed as the anode material of a lithium-ion battery. The structure and surface morphology were observed by SEM and XRD. The material was assembled into a button battery and a power battery for performance testing and safety testing. The experimental results indicate that the prepared soft carbon material has excellent surface properties and a uniform shape distribution. The first coulombic efficiency of the button battery is 87.5%. The charging capacity and discharging capacity ratios at 2C/1C are 84.8% and 85.9%, respectively. The capacity retention rate after 50 cycles at 0.5C is 99.9%. The discharge capacity of the battery decreases with increasing discharge ratio. After 500 cycles at 0.5C, 93.96% of the capacity is maintained. In low-temperature testing at -20oC, the discharge rate is 98.94%, while in high-temperature testing at 60°C, the discharge rate is 99.74%. The battery does not catch fire under needle punching, extrusion or overcharging. Therefore, the new soft carbon battery has excellent working performance and can be used as a new energy storage battery.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium ion battery
Soft carbon anode
Cyclic performance
Working performance

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS This work was supported by the National Key Research and Development Program of China (No. 2017YFB0102004) and Shandong Province Key Research and Development Program (No. 2017CXGC0502).

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Preparation and Electrochemical Performance of Spherical Mesophase Soft Carbon Materials Composite Anodes for Lithium-ion Batteries Hao Lin, 1 Qinghai Zhao, 2 Hongxin Zhang, 1 2 qduzhx@126.com 1 School of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China School of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering Qingdao University Qingdao 266071 China School of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China 2 Power Integration and Energy Storage System Engineering and Technology Center, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China Power Integration and Energy Storage System Engineering and Technology Center Qingdao University Qingdao 266071 China Power Integration and Energy Storage System Engineering and Technology Center, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China It is imperative to develop lithium battery anode materials with high power performance. In this paper, by improving the preparation process, a novel micro-nano composite structure spherical mesophase soft carbon material was developed as the anode material of a lithium-ion battery. The structure and surface morphology were observed by SEM and XRD. The material was assembled into a button battery and a power battery for performance testing and safety testing. The experimental results indicate that the prepared soft carbon material has excellent surface properties and a uniform shape distribution. The first coulombic efficiency of the button battery is 87.5%. The charging capacity and discharging capacity ratios at 2C/1C are 84.8% and 85.9%, respectively. The capacity retention rate after 50 cycles at 0.5C is 99.9%. The discharge capacity of the battery decreases with increasing discharge ratio. After 500 cycles at 0.5C, 93.96% of the capacity is maintained. In low-temperature testing at -20o C, the discharge rate is 98.94%, while in high-temperature testing at 60Â°C, the discharge rate is 99.74%. The battery does not catch fire under needle punching, extrusion or overcharging. Therefore, the new soft carbon battery has excellent working performance and can be used as a new energy storage battery.Keywords Lithium ion battery Soft carbon anode Cyclic performance Working performance References 1. simple-head.ce:title: Preparation and Electrochemical Performance of Spherical Mesophase Soft Carbon Materials Composite Anodes for Lithium-ion Batteries

ce:textfn.ce:italic: School of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China

ce:textfn.ce:italic: Power Integration and Energy Storage System Engineering and Technology Center, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Power Integration and Energy Storage System Engineering and Technology Center

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Power Integration and Energy Storage System Engineering and Technology Center, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: It is imperative to develop lithium battery anode materials with high power performance. In this paper, by improving the preparation process, a novel micro-nano composite structure spherical mesophase soft carbon material was developed as the anode material of a lithium-ion battery. The structure and surface morphology were observed by SEM and XRD. The material was assembled into a button battery and a power battery for performance testing and safety testing. The experimental results indicate that the prepared soft carbon material has excellent surface properties and a uniform shape distribution. The first coulombic efficiency of the button battery is 87.5%. The charging capacity and discharging capacity ratios at 2C/1C are 84.8% and 85.9%, respectively. The capacity retention rate after 50 cycles at 0.5C is 99.9%. The discharge capacity of the battery decreases with increasing discharge ratio. After 500 cycles at 0.5C, 93.96% of the capacity is maintained. In low-temperature testing at -20oC, the discharge rate is 98.94%, while in high-temperature testing at 60°C, the discharge rate is 99.74%. The battery does not catch fire under needle punching, extrusion or overcharging. Therefore, the new soft carbon battery has excellent working performance and can be used as a new energy storage battery.
